public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator.
manner: Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a quiet-room mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quiet-room mediator in and around Silkweaver’s Chapel, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Silkweaver’s Chapel workable through the obligations of quiet-room mediator while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must keep their piece of the world working while larger systems push stress downward. Around Silkweaver’s Chapel, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of quiet-room mediator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Taren Mere can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Taren Mere can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Taren Mere carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Silkweaver’s Chapel: the part of Silkweaver’s Chapel where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break
